question: Refer to Example 12.16. Analyze the distribution of the residuals in the grandfather clock example, both before and after the outlier residual is removed. Determine whether the assumption of a normally distributed error term is reasonable.
answer: A histogram and normal probability plot for the two sets of residuals are constructed using Minitab and are shown in Figures 12.45 and 12.46. Note that the outlier appears to skew the histogram in Figure 12.45, whereas the histogram in Figure 12.46 appears to be more mound-shaped. Similarly, the pattern of residuals in the normal probability plot in Figure 12.46 (outlier deleted) is more nearly a straight line than the pattern in Figure 12.45 (outlier included). Thus, the normality assumption appears to be more plausible after the outlier is removed.

meta_info.caption: Using Residuals to Check for Normal Errors-Grandfather Clock Prices
meta_info.section: 12.11 Residual Analysis: Checking the Regression Assumptions
